slides.headline: 5 Steps to Be Ready for a Compliance Survey
slides.body: Ensure your COE forms are in order and your 22-1999 is up to date.

slides.headline: Verify Your Certificate of Eligibility
slides.body: Check your student’s COE to ensure all necessary details are accurate and complete.
bullets: Verify the student’s eligibility for the Chapter 33 program

slides.headline: Ensure 22-1999 is Current
slides.body: Verify the 22-1999 form is completed and up to date, reflecting any recent changes in the student’s educational status.
bullets: Check the form against the most recent SAA letter

slides.headline: Review Chapter 33 Eligibility
slides.body: Confirm the student meets all Chapter 33 eligibility requirements, including the 85/15 rule.
bullets: Check the student’s Chapter 33 benefits are still valid

slides.headline: Check Yellow Ribbon Program Eligibility
slides.body: Ensure the student is eligible for the Yellow Ribbon Program, if applicable.
bullets: Review the student’s Yellow Ribbon enrollment status
Confirm the student meets the program’s criteria

slides.headline: Prepare for the Survey
slides.body: Have all necessary documentation ready, including student files and COE forms, to facilitate the audit process.
bullets: Be prepared to answer questions about your COE process

slides.body: Learn more about preparing for a VA audit. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Stay ahead of a compliance audit with these 5 essential steps. Verify your COE, 22-1999, Chapter 33 eligibility, Yellow Ribbon, and prepare for the survey. Learn more in our full guide. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 4, which could benefit from a more concise headline to improve readability and focus.
